data.title: HF Sinclair, PBF Energy downgraded at Wells Fargo on 2025 outlook
data.description:
Mid-cap refiners HF Sinclair (NYSE:DINO) and PBF Energy (NYSE:PBF) are downgraded Monday at Equal Weight from Overweight with respective $45 and $34 price targets at Wells Fargo, even as the bank remains positive overall on U.S. refining (CRAK) while anticipating "a year of two halves" for the industry - weaker up front, stronger in the back.

Wells Fargo analyst Roger Read says HF Sinclair (DINO) has done a solid job transforming to a more operational and geographically diverse company, but the continued weak performance of its RD operations is a bit of a headwind for improved earnings and returns.

PBF Energy (PBF) looks inexpensive but Read sees minimal prospects for wider crude differentials through mid-2025 as OPEC is likely to restrain production through mid-year; the analyst continues to view PBF's improved balance sheet and key position on the West Coast as advantaged and direct exposure to the East Coast remains a positive given below five-year average distillate inventory levels.

The Wells Fargo team reiterates Outperform ratings for large-cap refiners Marathon Petroleum (MPC), Phillips 66 (PSX) and Valero Energy (VLO).

data.title: Zeta Global tumbles on Interpublic-Omnicom merger; CEO issues statement
data.description:
• Zeta Global (NYSE:ZETA) shares tumbled more than 10% on Monday after Monday's mega-merger in the advertising sector between Interpublic (NYSE:IPG) and Omnicom (NYSE:OMC), prompting Zeta chief David Steinberg to issue a statement.
  • “Expanding on my earlier comments, we are proud of our extensive relationships with the top Holdcos, including both Omnicom and IPG, and believe that today’s announcement is a positive one for the industry and Zeta," Steinberg said. "Like everyone else, we will be watching closely as this progresses and offer support as needed.”

data.title: OpenAI launches Sora, AI-powered video creator
data.description:
Microsoft-backed (NASDAQ:MSFT) OpenAI launched Sora, an artificial intelligence-powered video generator that can create videos with as little as a few words of text.

"Video is important to OpenAi for a lot of reasons," said OpenAI CEO Sam Altman. "This is a new co-creative dynamic for our creators. We want our AI to understand video creations, and it will get better and better as it learns."

"This is going live today in most of the world, except for Europe and the U.K.," Altman added. "It might be a while before we can operate it there."

Users start on the Explore page, which is a feed of videos shared by the community. A box at the bottom of each video explains how it was created. Videos can be created via a line of text or using an image to kickstart the project.

There is also a more advanced tool called Storyboard. The creator can arrange a series of cards, with each card describing a specific scene. Storyboard then melds these instructions together into a video.

Sora was launched on Day 3 of "12 days of OpenAI."

On Day 1, OpenAI announced ChatGPT Pro while Day 2 highlighted its Reinforcement Fine-Tuning Research Program.

data.title: Retail 2025: Inventory strategies are crucial with port strikes, higher tariffs in the mix
data.description:
Major container ports in the U.S. are expected to see a continued surge in imports through next spring due to concerns about strikes in January at East Coast and Gulf Coast container ports and President-elect Donald Trump's plans to increase tariffs, according to the Global Port Tracker report released today by the National Retail Federation.

Talks have broken down between the International Longshoremen’s Association and the U.S. Maritime Alliance, leaving the potential for a strike after the current contract extension reached after a three-day strike in October expires January 15. Also in January, Donald Trump will return to the White House, which could lead to tariffs that boost prices on consumer products in certain categories.

"Either a strike or new tariffs would be a blow to the economy, and retailers are doing what they can to avoid the impact of either for as long as they can," stated NRF Vice President for Supply Chain and Customs Policy Jonathan Gold. The NRF called on both parties at the ports to return to the table, get a labor deal done and avoid a strike, and asked the incoming administration to use tariffs in a strategic manner rather than a broad-based approach impacting everyday consumer goods.

U.S. ports covered by Global Port Tracker handled 2.25 million Twenty-Foot Equivalent Units – one 20-foot container or its equivalent – in October, although the Port of Miami has yet to report final data. That was down 1.2% from September, but up 9.3% year over year. The forecast for January 2025 is 2.2 million TEU, up 12% year over year.

The dual threat could prompt major retailers such as Walmart (WMT), Target (TGT), Costco (COST), Best Buy (BBY), Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS), Five Below (FIVE), Dollar General (DG), and Dollar Tree (DLTR) to pull in spring inventory early.

data.title: YPF upgraded to Buy at BofA, saying not too late to join the party
data.description:
YPF (NYSE:YPF) +5% in Monday's trading as Bank of America upgrades shares to Buy from Neutral with a $55 price target, even after the stock has more than doubled in the past year as Argentina's country risk fell to its lowest level since 2019.

Meanwhile, YPF's (YPF) micro story has advanced with divestment of conventional assets as well as its liquefied natural gas integrated product, which BofA believes will continue to support the stock.

YPF (YPF) already has signed the sale of more than 25 blocks out of 50 of conventional assets, representing less than 1% of the company's EBITDA and requiring ~$800M in capex, so BofA analysts say the divestment of these areas is highly accretive for the company and will allow YPF to focus on Vaca Muerta, which offers much higher returns.

BofA notes the news flow regarding integrated LNG also has increased substantially, indicating that YPF's (YPF) important project - for both the company and Argentina - could start to be developed soon.

YPF's (YPF) recently confirmed it will join a joint venture formed by Pan American Energy and Golar LNG to export LNG from Vaca Muerta to the world starting in 2027 and thus insert Argentina into the market.

data.title: SLM downgraded to Market Perform at KBW
data.description:
Keefe, Bruyette & Woods downgraded SLM Corp. (NASDAQ:SLM), formerly known as Sallie Mae, to Market Perform from Outperform after the stock surged 43% YTD, making the accretion math "tougher" around selling student loans at a premium and buying back stock.

"While a lower rate environment should benefit the gain on sale margins, the level of benefit could be offset by a higher level of consolidation activity away from SLM (NASDAQ:SLM) as we have seen in the past," KBW analyst Sanjay Sakhrani wrote in a note to clients.

He also said the impact of Trump 2.0 on SLM's business remains unclear as student loan forgiveness programs have historically affected federal government student loans and not private student loans, which is the basis of SLM's business.

Furthermore, talk about shutting down the Department of Education is unlikely to be implemented as congressional approval would be required, and Republicans only hold the House by a very narrow margin.

SLM (SLM) stock fell 1.4% in Monday afternoon trading.

KBW's Market Perform rating on SLM (SLM) aligns with the SA Quant rating of Hold and contrasts with the average Wall Street rating of Strong Buy.

data.title: Oshkosh could lose USPS contract to electrify fleet - update
data.description:
Updated 12/9 14:20ET with company response.

Shares of Oshkosh Corporation (NYSE:OSK) were broad-sided on Friday as reports circulated that the incoming Trump administration would cancel the U.S. Postal Service’s contract with electric vehicle manufacturers including Oshkosh (OSK) and Ford Motor (F).

According to Reuters, the measure is part of a broader initiative by the incoming administration to reverse government subsidies enacted by President Biden to encourage the adoption of electric vehicles.

Sources cited by Reuters say Trump’s transition team is reviewing options to unwind the postal service’s multi-billion-dollar contracts with companies like Oshkosh (OSK) and Ford (F) to provide tens of thousands of BEVs and charging stations.

The postal service was awarded a $3B grant from the U.S. government last year to electrify its fleet with 66,000 EVs by 2028, of which 45,000 would come from Oshkosh (OSK), and the remainder from other automakers. The charging stations would be provided by ChargePoint (CHPT), Blink Charging (BLNK) and Siemens (OTCPK:SIEGY).

Severing these contracts would be “legally challenging,” Reuters says, as the USPS operates as an autonomous federal agency with its own governing board. Therefore, any measures to rescind government contracts would likely require an Executive Order from the incoming president.

"New vehicles are in service today...and have received overwhelming positive feedback from Postal Carriers," Oshkosh spokesperson Tim Gilman said to Seeking Alpha.

"Oshkosh is fully committed to our strong partnership with USPS and looks forward to continuing to provide our Postal Carriers with reliable, safe, and sustainable modern delivery vehicles, even as USPS's needs continue to evolve," Gilman added.

data.title: Biogen downgraded to hold by Jefferies, Ocrevus royalties cited
data.description:
Biogen (NASDAQ:BIIB) was downgraded to hold from buy by Jefferies, due in part to a looming royalty cut for the multiple sclerosis drug Ocrevus.

Jefferies said it downgraded the stock on three issues: anticipated modest sales of Leqembi, lack of a pipeline that enthuses investors, and an expected 50% step down in royalties for Ocrevus in 2030.

The investment bank added that Biogen (BIIB) needs more pipeline candidates and business development to help offset the 2030 step down for Ocrevus.

Ocrevus is marketed by Roche’s (OTCQX:RHHBY) Genentech subsidiary.

Jefferies lowered its price target for Biogen (BIIB) to $180 from $250.

data.title: Warner Bros. Discovery enters content sharing agreement with Comcast
data.description:
Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD) has entered a multi-year deal with Comcast (NASDAQ:CMCSA) to provide its portfolio of content to Xfinity and Sky UK and Ireland subscribers, along with continued carriage of HBO, and expand Comcast’s right to package the ad-supported versions of Max and Discovery+ within its streaming bundles. Comcast (CMCSA) will also continue to offer Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) content as part of its U.S. NOW TV streaming service.

“Through these agreements, we will bring Warner Bros. Discovery’s extensive portfolio to our customers however they want to consume the content across our existing and future linear television and streaming bundles,” said Comcast’s president of contact acquisition.

The Warner Bros. Discovery/Comcast agreement will also include a new, non-exclusive ad-supported Max app when WBD launches the service in the UK and Ireland in early 2026.

According to Deadline, the deal appears to settle what was a “contentious” situation in the UK, where Max is set to roll out into Sky territory in 2026 amid an ongoing international expansion, and after its HBO output deal with Sky expires. Last September, Comcast (CMCSA) sued Warner Bros. (WBD) for failing to comply with the terms of its five-year output deal between HBO and Sky in the UK, Italy and Germany. By agreeing to the above terms, the lawsuit against WBD is now moot.

Financial terms of the deal were not disclosed.

Shares of Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) were propped up to an 11-month-high.

data.title: Man questioned in Pennsylvania in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO: reports (update)
